notes.text:
# Release Notes for Version 0.12.0

We are pleased to announce the release of Version 0.12.0 of our software. This update brings significant enhancements and changes designed to improve the performance, security, and stability of your applications. It includes the addition of a new feature for gRPC API server and namespace isolation, and an update to our documentation reflecting the acceptance of ADR 0001.

## New Features

### Improved Communication with gRPC API Server

In this release, we have added a new feature for the gRPC API server to work alongside HTTP REST. This allows for a more efficient, high-performance mechanism for communication between services. This enhancement will enable faster data exchanges, reduce latency, and provide a more streamlined development experience.

### Enhanced Security with Namespace Isolation

We have also introduced namespace isolation for SQLite and memory providers in the store. This new feature will help in isolating resources and reducing the risk of accidental interference. By effectively segregating resources, this feature improves the security and stability of your applications, ensuring that they run smoothly and safely.

## Changes

### Acceptance of ADR 0001

As part of our ongoing commitment to transparency and clarity, we have updated our documentation to mark ADR 0001 as "Accepted". This decision has been implemented since version 0.11.0. This update provides a more accurate representation of the project's design decisions, aiding developers in understanding the rationale behind certain features and implementations.

This release reflects our commitment to continuous improvement and our dedication to providing the best possible tools for our developers. We encourage you to update to Version 0.12.0 at your earliest convenience to take advantage of these improvements. As always, your feedback is greatly appreciated and will help shape our future releases.

## Changelog

## [0.12.0] - 2021-MM-DD

### Added
- A new feature for gRPC API server alongside HTTP REST. This addition provides a more efficient, high-performance mechanism for communication between services.
- A new feature for namespace isolation for SQLite and memory providers in the store. This will help in isolating resources and reducing the risk of accidental interference, thereby improving both the security and stability of the applications.

### Changed
- The ADR 0001 has been marked as "Accepted" in the documentation. It has been implemented since version 0.11.0. This update reflects the current status of the ADR, providing developers with a more accurate understanding of the project's design decisions.

commits.subject: feat: gRPC API server alongside HTTP REST
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat: gRPC API server alongside HTTP REST

Add a full gRPC API surface to Mnemos mirroring the existing HTTP REST
endpoints. Enables high-performance service-to-service communication
for the cognitive stack (Chronos, Praxis, Nous).

Proto definitions (proto/mnemos/v1/mnemos.proto):
- Health, Events, Claims, Relationships, Embeddings, Metrics services
- Pagination, filtering, and batch append support
- google.protobuf.Timestamp for native time handling

Generated code:
- proto/gen/mnemos/v1/mnemos.pb.go
- proto/gen/mnemos/v1/mnemos_grpc.pb.go
- Makefile target: make proto

Server implementation (internal/server/grpc/server.go):
- Implements mnemosv1.MnemosServiceServer
- Reuses port-typed repositories from *store.Conn
- Bearer-token auth via gRPC metadata (same JWT verifier as HTTP)
- Read methods allow anonymous access; writes require scopes
- Panic recovery and structured access logging via bolt
- Auth disabled when verifier is nil (useful for tests/embedded)

CLI integration (cmd/mnemos/serve.go):
- New --grpc-port flag: mnemos serve --grpc-port 50051
- Shares store.Conn and auth verifier with HTTP surface
- Graceful shutdown on both HTTP and gRPC servers

Tests (internal/server/grpc/server_test.go):
- Health, Events, Claims, Relationships, Embeddings, Metrics round-trips
- Validation error coverage (empty arrays, missing IDs)
- Uses memory:// provider for hermetic tests

commits.subject: refactor(store): remove dead nsKey helper and namespace field from memory provider
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
refactor(store): remove dead nsKey helper and namespace field from memory provider

The memory provider already isolates by giving each Conn its own
fresh state instance. The nsKey helper and namespace field were
never called — they implied key-prefix isolation that didn't exist.
Keep namespace parsing/validation in openProvider for DSN consistency
across all providers.

commits.subject: docs(adr): mark ADR 0001 as Accepted (implemented in v0.11.0)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message: docs(adr): mark ADR 0001 as Accepted (implemented in v0.11.0)

commits.subject: feat(store): namespace isolation for SQLite and memory providers
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(store): namespace isolation for SQLite and memory providers

SQLite provider:
- Parse ?namespace= from DSN query params (default: mnemos)
- Validate namespace against ADR 0001 §3 regex
- Non-default namespaces produce distinct files (e.g. db_chronos.db)
- Backward compatible: default namespace preserves original path

Memory provider:
- Parse ?namespace= from DSN query params
- Validate namespace, store in state
- Add nsKey helper for future shared-state support

Tests:
- SQLite provider_test.go: DSN parsing with/without namespace
- memory_test.go: default, explicit, and invalid namespace handling
